title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Google hit with massive EU fine
slides.explanation: Google has been ordered to pay 4.1 billion euros for using its Android system to block competitors. A company spokesperson argued the ruling ignores how much they have invested to keep the platform open.

slides.headline: AI is hurting green goals
slides.explanation: Tech giants like Amazon and Google are finding it harder to meet their net-zero climate promises. The energy required to run AI systems is becoming a major hurdle for these companies.

slides.headline: Is current AI actually smart?
slides.explanation: Leading AI researcher Yann LeCun says current AI systems are not as smart as people think. His new start-up is now working on a more flexible way for machines to learn.

slides.headline: Microsoft bets big on AI
slides.explanation: Microsoft is launching a new group dedicated to AI deployment with a 2.5 billion dollar commitment. They are joining other major players like Amazon and OpenAI in this push.

slides.headline: Meta's new app called Pocket
slides.explanation: Meta is launching an app called Pocket, but it has nothing to do with the old read-it-later tool. This version uses AI prompts to let users create and share interactive digital gizmos.

caption: From massive antitrust fines to the environmental cost of AI, it has been a busy day in the tech world. We are also seeing new investments in AI deployment and some fresh takes on what 'smart' technology really means. Get the full story on all these headlines in the Headlinne app. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Liberal MP launches legal challenge
slides.explanation: Victorian Liberal MP Moira Deeming is taking legal action against her own party president. The move comes right before a meeting meant to decide her future within the party.

slides.headline: Rare 1776 document found in London
slides.explanation: The UK National Archives has discovered a rare copy of the US Declaration of Independence. It was found among the papers of a ship that was captured back in 1776.

slides.headline: Judge wants more details on Trump golf plan
slides.explanation: A federal judge is asking for stronger guarantees regarding the Trump administration's plans for a DC golf course. The court wants firmer commitments before moving forward with the project.

slides.headline: Experts debunk Trump's communist claims
slides.explanation: Experts say recent claims by President Trump linking Democrats to communism are not accurate. The fact check addresses the President's warnings about the threat of communism to the US.

slides.headline: Video evidence in Pooh Shiesty case
slides.explanation: Prosecutors are using a video that appears to show rapper Pooh Shiesty demanding to be released from his record label. The footage is being presented as part of the ongoing case against him.

caption: From a rare historical find in London to ongoing legal developments in US politics, here are five stories you should know today. Catch up on the latest headlines quickly and easily with Headlinne. Check out the full stories in the app. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
